title: senecio malacitanus subsp. frigidus
intro: Senecio malacitanus subsp. frigidus, also known as the Canary Island ragwort or Canary ragwort, is a subspecies of plant in the sunflower family native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs and congestion, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Senecio malacitanus subsp. frigidus typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
